description: Unify the retro Menu dropdown styling with the retro Select dropdown so all dropdowns in the side panel share the same visual language. The Menu.Content and Menu.Item styles must match Select.Content and Select.Item — same border weight, background, padding, font weight, and hover colors. The PluginMenu destructive item (Remove/Uninstall) should use destructive color on hover with proper theme integration instead of ad-hoc class overrides. Update all Menu stories and PluginMenu stories to verify visual consistency.

userStories.title: Unify Menu.Content and Menu.Item styling with Select equivalents
userStories.description: Update the retro Menu primitive so its Content and Item styles match the Select primitive: same border weight, background color, item padding, and font weight. Update the destructive Menu.Item pattern to use theme-consistent colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu.Content uses border (1px) instead of border-2, matching Select.Content
Menu.Content uses bg-background instead of bg-card, matching Select.Content
Menu.Content does NOT use a fixed w-56 width — uses min-w fitting content or a reasonable min-w
Menu.Item uses px-2 py-1.5 padding, matching Select.Item
Menu.Item highlight uses focus:bg-primary focus:text-primary-foreground and data-[highlighted]:bg-primary data-[highlighted]:text-primary-foreground, matching Select.Item

PluginMenu destructive item uses text-destructive with hover bg-destructive/10 and text-destructive (no raw class overrides on the consumer — add a variant prop or a built-in destructive class to Menu.Item)
All existing Menu consumers (PluginMenu, BrowserToolsMenu) render correctly with the updated styles
Menu.stories.tsx WithSeparator story shows the destructive item with proper styling
PluginMenu.stories.tsx VersionInMenu play test still passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.tsx (update Content and Item styles)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.stories.tsx (update stories)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx (clean up ad-hoc destructive classes)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.stories.tsx (verify stories still work)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsMenu.tsx (verify no breakage)

Context:

The Select primitive (Select.tsx) defines the visual standard for dropdowns:

Select.Content (Select.tsx:25-51):
```tsx
<SelectPrimitive.Content
  className={cn(
    'data-[state=closed]:fade-out-0 data-[state=open]:fade-in-0 data-[state=closed]:zoom-out-95 data-[state=open]:zoom-in-95 data-[side=bottom]:slide-in-from-top-2 data-[side=left]:slide-in-from-right-2 data-[side=right]:slide-in-from-left-2 data-[side=top]:slide-in-from-bottom-2 relative z-50 min-w-[8rem] overflow-hidden border border-border bg-background text-foreground shadow-md data-[state=closed]:animate-out data-[state=open]:animate-in',
    ...
  )}
```
Key classes: `border border-border bg-background shadow-md`

Select.Item (Select.tsx:55-70):
```tsx
<SelectPrimitive.Item
  className={cn(
    'relative flex w-full cursor-default select-none items-center px-2 py-1.5 outline-none focus:bg-primary focus:text-primary-foreground data-disabled:pointer-events-none data-[highlighted]:bg-primary data-[highlighted]:text-primary-foreground data-disabled:opacity-50',
    className,
  )}
```
Key classes: `px-2 py-1.5`, highlight uses `bg-primary text-primary-foreground`

The Menu primitive (Menu.tsx) currently differs:

Menu.Content (Menu.tsx:9-23):
```tsx
<DropdownMenuPrimitive.Content
  ref={ref}
  sideOffset={sideOffset}
  className={cn('z-50 w-56 rounded border-2 border-border bg-card shadow-md', className)}
  {...props}
/>
```
Differences: `w-56` (fixed width), `border-2` (2px), `bg-card`, `rounded`

Menu.Item (Menu.tsx:25-34):
```tsx
<DropdownMenuPrimitive.Item
  ref={ref}
  className={cn(
    'relative flex cursor-pointer select-none items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1.5 font-sans text-foreground text-sm outline-none transition-colors focus:bg-primary focus:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:pointer-events-none data-[highlighted]:bg-primary data-[highlighted]:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:opacity-50 [&_svg:not([class*=size-])]:size-4 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0',
    className,
  )}
```
Differences: `gap-2 px-3` (wider padding), `cursor-pointer`, `font-sans text-sm`, `transition-colors`

Changes:

1. **Menu.Content** — update className to match Select.Content:
```tsx
className={cn('z-50 min-w-[8rem] overflow-hidden border border-border bg-background shadow-md', className)}
```
Removed: `w-56` (fixed width), `border-2` (now `border`), `bg-card` (now `bg-background`), `rounded` (Select doesn't use it either, and theme radius is 0 so it has no effect, but remove for consistency).

2. **Menu.Item** — update className to match Select.Item padding and keep the icon SVG helpers:
```tsx
className={cn(
  'relative flex cursor-default select-none items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1.5 text-foreground text-sm outline-none focus:bg-primary focus:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:pointer-events-none data-[highlighted]:bg-primary data-[highlighted]:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:opacity-50 [&_svg:not([class*=size-])]:size-4 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0',
  className,
)}
```
Changes: `px-3` → `px-2`, `cursor-pointer` → `cursor-default` (matches Select), removed `font-sans` (inherits), removed `transition-colors` (Select doesn't use it).

3. **Menu.Item destructive variant** — Add a `variant` prop to MenuItem:
```tsx
const MenuItem = ({ className, variant, ref, ...props }: ComponentProps<typeof DropdownMenuPrimitive.Item> & { variant?: 'destructive' }) => (
  <DropdownMenuPrimitive.Item
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      'relative flex cursor-default select-none items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1.5 text-foreground text-sm outline-none focus:bg-primary focus:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:pointer-events-none data-[highlighted]:bg-primary data-[highlighted]:text-primary-foreground data-[disabled]:opacity-50 [&_svg:not([class*=size-])]:size-4 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0',
      variant === 'destructive' && 'text-destructive focus:bg-destructive/10 focus:text-destructive data-[highlighted]:bg-destructive/10 data-[highlighted]:text-destructive',
      className,
    )}
    {...props}
  />
);
```

4. **PluginMenu.tsx** — Clean up the destructive item. Replace:
```tsx
<Menu.Item
  onSelect={() => setConfirmOpen(true)}
  className="text-destructive focus:bg-destructive/10 focus:text-destructive data-[highlighted]:bg-destructive/10 data-[highlighted]:text-destructive">
```
With:
```tsx
<Menu.Item
  onSelect={() => setConfirmOpen(true)}
  variant="destructive">
```

5. **Menu.stories.tsx** — Update WithSeparator story to use `variant="destructive"` instead of raw className override:
```tsx
<Menu.Item variant="destructive">Delete</Menu.Item>
```

6. **BrowserToolsMenu.tsx** — No changes needed. It only uses a disabled Menu.Item for the version display. Verify it still looks correct with the new padding.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the Select primitive — it is the reference design. Only Menu changes to match it.
- The `gap-2` on Menu.Item is needed because menu items have icons alongside text (unlike Select items which only have text + checkmark). Keep `gap-2`.
- The `[&_svg]` helpers on Menu.Item are needed for icon sizing in menu items. Keep them.
- Do NOT remove the `sideOffset` prop on Menu.Content — it controls dropdown offset from trigger.
- The Menu.Separator style (`-mx-1 my-1 h-px bg-border`) is fine — no change needed.
- The `overflow-hidden` on Content prevents item highlight from bleeding outside the border.
- Test in both light and dark themes. The bg-background token resolves correctly in both.

userStories.title: Add Storybook stories showing Menu and Select side-by-side for visual consistency verification
userStories.description: Add a composite Storybook story that renders a Menu dropdown and a Select dropdown side-by-side so visual consistency can be verified at a glance. Update PluginMenu stories to cover the version + update + destructive variant flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu.stories.tsx has a SideBySideWithSelect story showing a Menu and a PermissionSelect next to each other, both open or demonstrating the same visual language
PluginMenu.stories.tsx AllStates story is updated to show the destructive item using variant='destructive' (not raw className)
PluginMenu.stories.tsx has a story showing version + update available + destructive remove in one menu (all three sections visible)
PluginCard.stories.tsx stories still render correctly with the new Menu styling
